page: Microsoft Azure D2pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dplsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs powered by a Microsoft Neoverse-N2 ARM processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The system is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates a mix of average and weak performance profiles. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 2073.69 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1683.0, both falling into the average category. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable strength, registering at 33.08 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations are generally weak. The stress-ng multi-core score is 4127.28, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 3085.0. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is weak at 20.82 GB/sec, and the Passmark memory score is poor at 1050.11.

The primary tradeoff of the D2pls_v6 is its low memory allocation of 4.0 GB relative to its 2 dedicated vCPUs, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive applications. This is reflected in poor Passmark CPU encryption and extended instruction scores, as well as weak performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, basic database operations, and low-throughput web serving. Appropriate workloads include lightweight utility services, simple text processing, and basic database tasks that do not require high memory capacity or intensive multi-threaded compute.
description: Microsoft Azure D2pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dplsv6 family, configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. Powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor, this instance operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and lacks local storage and GPU resources. Performance benchmarks reveal a moderate single-core profile, highlighted by a strong single-threaded gzip compression speed of 33.08 GB/sec and average Geekbench single-core results. However, multi-core and memory-bound metrics are weak, with a Passmark memory score of 1050.11 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 3085.0. Given the low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this server is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic text processing, and low-concurrency database operations where high memory capacity is not required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Neoverse-N2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering moderate single-core performance for light workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2pls_v6 is an ARM-based VM with 2 dedicated vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and moderate single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine with dedicated dual-core processing and low memory density for lightweight single-threaded utility workloads.
bullet_points: Features 2 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 vCPUs operating at 3.4 GHz.
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and no local storage.
Delivers strong single-threaded gzip compression performance at 33.08 GB/sec.
Exhibits weak multi-core and memory-bound benchmark results.
Best suited for lightweight text processing, basic database operations, and utility services.
